observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Require the immediate liquidation of all cash advances, especially those aged one year and below; otherwise, require the employees who are still active in service to refund the unliquidated amount or withhold the same from any amount due them; and
Send demand letters to those resigned or retired officials and employees with long outstanding cash advances for immediate settlement.
observations.reason: Only few has liquidated their cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Immediately stop the practice of utilizing the PCF for payment of recurring expenses; and
Strictly utilize the PCF for payment of authorized non-recurring petty operating expenses which cannot be conveniently paid through checks.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Observe proper recording of transactions using appropriate accounts to ensure proper presentation of expense accounts in the financial statements; 
Submit the lacking documents and/or copies of official receipts in case of refunds to fully liquidate those expenses with insufficient liquidations; 
Stop the practice of granting donations and/or payment of advertisements to other LGUs relative to celebration of fiestas and to various religious groups; and
Stop the practice of paying membership and similar fees paid for personal or individual membership in a private organization.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Provide a stockroom for proper storage of procured items of inventories and require proper coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the GSO in the monitoring and recording of supplies and materials;
Require the GSO to prepare the SSMI weekly supported with duly accomplished RIS and the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV based on the SSMI to record the corresponding expenditures; and
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials on a semestral basis and submit a report thereon to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.
No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Verify the supporting documents of the recorded Accounts Payable account amounting to ₱376,316.20 to determine its validity.  If found to be valid, provide copies of the said supporting documents to the Audit Team for further validation, otherwise, make the necessary adjustments to derecognize the recorded payable accounts from the books of the LGU; and
Ensure that all transactions recorded as Accounts Payable are supported with proper and complete documentation to establish a valid claim.
observations.managementAction: Schedule of recorded accounts payable at year-end was provided to the Audit Team. Documents and DVs for the payment of the said accounts payable were included in the submitted January and February accounts.
observations.reason: Only the list or schedule of Accounts Payable was submitted to the audit team. Supporting documents were not provided for validation, therefore, the completeness and accuracy of recorded Accounts Payable as of December 2023 cannot be established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to revert the unexpended balances of appropriations for MOOE to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year.

We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit SCBAA within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended 20 percent DF were reprogrammed for new PPAs.
observations.reason:
The action taken does not cure the non-compliance with the prescribed guidelines. The unexpended balance of MOOE should first be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the GF at the end of fiscal year and shall not be thereafter available for expenditure except by subsequent enactment.

Verification revealed that the SCBAA for CY 2024 was still not submitted within the required period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Restate the accounts affected by prior period errors for all ensuing financial statements to fairly present the comparative financial statements of the LGU; and
Observe the required disclosures in the NTFS relative to the recorded prior period errors/adjustments.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the GSO-Designate to:

Conduct the required pre- and post-inspection of motor vehicles subjected to repair and prepare the corresponding reports thereon; and
Maintain a record of history of repair and maintenance of each motor vehicle owned and/or operated by the LGU; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents for the repair and maintenance of motor vehicles to support the payments made and for further evaluation of the Audit Team.  Likewise, ensure that payment of repairs and maintenance of each motor vehicle is duly supported with complete documentation.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the designated drivers to secure a duly approved driver’s trip ticket before using the government vehicle and properly accomplish the information on the said form.  Likewise, require them to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each government vehicle used; and
Direct the authorized personnel to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit the same to the Auditor for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.
observations.managementAction:
Driver’s Trip Ticket is issued upon but the personnel in charge in the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel is yet to be assigned.

Management will identify and assign personnel to prepare the report.
observations.reason: Driver’s Trip Ticket are still not properly and completely accomplished; Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each government vehicle remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of allowing claims of DTE beyond the allowable amount as provided in EO No. 77; and
Stop the practice of reimbursing the cost of fuel loaded to private vehicles. Instead, claim the allowable equivalent cost of customary mode of transportation in accordance with EO No. 77.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of paying through reimbursement, instead payment should be made directly to suppliers to ensure that the corresponding taxes are withheld; and
Strictly adhere to the method of procurement as required under RA No. 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained for the benefit of the agency.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the MPDC to:

Ensure proper planning of priority development projects and ensure that these projects are feasible to implement in the year these were budgeted; and
Closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of PPAs identified in the approved development plan; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all savings from completed projects are immediately considered for reversion and reappropriation to provide additional funding for other development projects.
observations.managementAction:
Management issued a letter to all concerned departments reminding their compliance with the audit recommendation.

Savings from implemented projects were reverted to the General Fund.
observations.reason: For further verification of the succeeding budget and PPA implementation; Some of the identified priority projects remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to request for snap shots from the bank every end of the month or register all the bank accounts in an online banking facility/system available in the depository banks of the LGU to obtain timely bank statements and furnish copy to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly BRS within the prescribed period;
Require the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries to take up the valid reconciling items in the books of accounts and identify the details of adjusting journal entries previously recorded, including the supporting documents, amounting to ₱872,330.92 to verify if such adjusting journal entries are proper and accurate, otherwise, make the necessary adjustments on affected accounts; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on a monthly basis or as often as needed to closely monitor the correctness of the balances of Cash in Bank account.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to request for write-off of dormant accounts that could no longer be accounted or settled following the required documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005;
Require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve all the necessary documents of the dormant receivable and payable accounts to be able to determine the age of fund transfer/grant and liability, and request for write-off if found to be non-moving for more than 10 years; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLs which contain the details of breakdown of the balance of the controlling account appearing in the GL.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to initially record all regular purchases of supplies and materials under the inventory account following the perpetual inventory method of accounting; require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the maintenance and updating of subsidiary ledger cards for supplies and materials; and require the Municipal Accountant and GSO- Designate to conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the Hospital and Health Centers account with a negative balance of ₱315,449.18 and make the necessary adjustment once accounted.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the GSO- Designate to prepare and update each PAR every three years after issue or every time there is a change in accountability.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.
No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the unexpended balances of LDRRMF to be accounted under the TF books for proper and easy monitoring and reconciliation of the recorded Trust Liabilities – DRRMF with the actual balance of cash in bank.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer to the TF account the amount corresponding to the share of the OBO from the collection of building permit fees; and
Municipal Engineering Office as the OBO to prepare a Program of Work detailing the proposed utilization of the 15 percent share from the collection of building permit fees to be approved by the Local Chief Executive.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to submit the duly approved CSHP of the eight infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Lacking documents were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC to ensure that before the awarding and signing of the contract, the winning bidder should post surety bond callable upon demand as performance security together with project specific certification from the Insurance Commission.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the GSO- Designate and the Inspectorate Team to ensure that the Acceptance and Inspection Reports are properly accomplished to fully establish the accountability; and
Direct the GSO- Designate to require the supplier to issue a duly dated delivery receipt with complete data upon delivery of goods in order for the former to acknowledge the receipt of goods by signing in the “Acceptance” portion thereof as well as the AIR.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the HRMO to ensure that all required supporting documents are attached to the payroll before effecting payment.
observations.reason: Same deficiency was noted in CY 2024 audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Submit the required documents for the repair and maintenance of equipment and motor vehicles for further evaluation of the Audit Team to support the payments made; and
Exercise prudence and diligence in his duties and responsibilities in the verification of the completeness of the documents for all transactions of the LGU before endorsing them for payment.
observations.reason: The majority of the documents were still not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the MSWDO to ensure that all financial assistance be supported with appropriate, valid and complete documentary requirements to avoid possible misuse or misapplication of funds; and
Direct the MSWDO to conduct a thorough screening and assessment by validating the submitted documents and other necessary means to confirm the qualifications of individuals seeking financial assistance, particularly those recurring clients.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MSWDO to exhaust all possible means to collect the amount received by the remaining five ineligible SAP beneficiaries totaling ₱27,500.00 and cause its immediate return to the DSWD FO1, San Fernando City, La Union.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop the practice of withholding the loan amortization of the employees on behalf of the private entities and the Municipal Treasurer and terminate immediately the arrangement with these private lending institutions.  For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management could not stop the withholding of loan amortization of the employees due to the existing MOA that was executed between the LGU and the private banks.
observations.reason: Deduction of loan amortization from the salaries of municipal employees for their existing loans was still enforced in accordance with the terms of the MOA. However, renewal of such loans was not allowed by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating and charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation.  Proper coordination must be observed among municipal officials and employees in order that expenses are obligated and taken up as Accounts Payable in the year these were incurred and payment thereof is made in the succeeding year.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the missing disbursement vouchers and supporting documents totaling ₱65,583.96 to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books and submit the same to the Audit Team for proper validation of the transactions. Otherwise, require the Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount of unsubmitted vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to submit the missing disbursement vouchers totaling ₱65,583.96.
observations.reason: Disbursement vouchers and supporting documents were not yet submitted the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the details of the difference arising from the reconciliation of SCBAA and SFPer under Personal Services and MOOE totaling ₱1,991,478.04 and ₱35,343.77, respectively;
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to maintain RAAOs and SAAOB, respectively, in support of the actual amounts in the SCBAA and the reconciliation with the amounts presented in the SFPer; and
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to revert the unspent MOOE under 20% development fund to the unappropriated surplus subject for re-programming in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly follow the procurement guidelines in the lease of venue for seminars to be conducted and submit immediately the lacking documents mentioned above to the Audit Team for verification and review to determine the legality and propriety of the transaction.
observations.reason: Lacking documents were not yet submitted to the Audit Team for further evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly accomplish all purchase orders to provide the necessary information and basis in determining the reliability of the procurement process in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010 and MNGAS for LGUs and submit to the Audit Team within five (5) days after perfection thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to properly accomplish the purchase orders and submit the same within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to instruct the Local School Board to prepare the SEF Budget in accordance with the schedule of activities as provided under DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.1, S. 2017;
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies to improve the collection from delinquent market stall holders.
observations.reason: No action has been taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue sending notices to delinquent taxpayers and to conduct information drive campaign especially on the discount given for advance payment in order to encourage prompt payment and to generate more revenue for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2024, delinquent property taxes significantly increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with the identified GAD-related issues and concerns so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided by the GAD Program.
observations.reason: Validation cannot be made since GAD Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Report were not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee purposely for the conduct of the one-time cleansing.

We also recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Promptly prepare the lapsing schedule to support the computation of depreciation expense;
Exert utmost effort to reconcile with the GSO records to secure the existence of the PPE owned and controlled by the LGU;
Direct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties at least once a year, and to subsequently prepare the RPCPPE and submit a copy to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, GSO, and the Inventory Committee to comply with the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, and to ensure regular coordination and reconciliation of records to avoid discrepancies and reflect reliable balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Inventory and Disposal Committee of the LGU and the Appraisal Committee or the equivalent to initiate and facilitate the appraisal of the unserviceable properties; and
General Services Officer-Designate, in coordination with the Inventory and Disposal Committee of the LGU, as well as other concerned personnel to:
Facilitate the preparation and/or finalize the IIRUP with provision of reasonable appraisal value of those unserviceable properties and promptly cause the disposal taking into consideration the appropriate and most advantageous mode of disposal following the conditions and procedures provided in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties; and
Submit the IIRUP duly supported with the necessary documents to the Accounting Office upon completion of disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries as presented below to reclassify the semi-expendable properties issued and to  derecognize the depreciation expenses previously recorded in the books; and
Prepare adjusting journal entries to derecognize the semi-expendable items purchased prior to CY 2024 which were recognized as PPE.
Direct the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries, and reports to strengthen the controls over the semi-expendable property.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the proper recording of insurance premium payments by recognizing them initially as prepaid expenses and subsequently amortizing them over the coverage period. Accordingly, the necessary adjusting entry should be made to reflect the correct balances by debiting Prepaid Insurance in the amount of ₱53,956.25 and crediting Insurance Expenses in the same amount.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Restate the accounts affected by prior period errors for all ensuing financial statements to fairly present the comparative financial statements of the LGU; and
Observe the required disclosures in the NTFS relative to the recorded prior period errors/adjustments.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC Chairperson through the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of perfected contracts, approved POs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five working days upon perfection of the contract pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a timely review and evaluation thereof.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to secure and attach the original copies of perfected contracts along with their supporting documents as attachments to the DVs to assure completeness of documents and regularity of transactions.

We recommended that Management require the concerned accountable officers to observe the prescribed period in the submission of accounts and financial reports for timely communication of any deficiency that may be noted in audit which may be relevant in decision making.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required monthly BRS within 10 days after the receipt of snapshots and/or bank statements.

We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required monthly pre-closing trial balance and its corresponding schedules as well as the SAAOB for each fund on or before the 20th day after the end of the month.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat, upon receipt of the PR, to strictly check and verify whether the items requested are with reference to brand names or with tailor-fitted specifications attributed to specific brand. If so, return the PR to the requesting unit for correction and modification.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the BAC to stop the practice of delegating preparation of POs to the end users or the requesting officials;
Instruct the BAC Chairman, in coordination with the BAC Secretariat/Procurement Unit, to ensure the correctness and completeness of information in the Purchase Orders to support its validity and protect the interest of the government in case of the suppliers’ failure to render services or deliver goods in accordance with the approved POs; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly evaluate the completeness and accuracy of the information, as well as the sufficiency and propriety of the submitted documentary requirements prior to certification.

We recommended that management direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to stop the practice of granting requests for monetization of 50 percent accumulated leave credits without valid and justifiable reason; and
We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to henceforth ensure that monetization of leave credits be supported with complete documentation before approving the payment thereof.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Monitor and ensure timely liquidation of the cash advances within the required period, send demand letters and strictly enforce the withholding of salaries for officials and employees who fail to fully liquidate their cash advances within the required period, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer;
Ensure that no additional cash advance shall be granted to AOs with an outstanding unliquidated cash advance; and
Submit the liquidation report and the supporting documents of the cash advances totaling ₱2,168,620.00 wherein the liquidation thereof was already recorded in the books.  In the absence of sufficient supporting documents, reverse the entry recording the actual expenses to ensure proper accountability by the AOs.
We also recommended that Management ensure to limit and designate, and properly bond SDOs whose duties permit or require the possession or custody of government funds in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials and employees to refund the amounts of ₱53,000.00 and ₱12,600.00, representing the excessive registration fees and travel claims, respectively.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the concerned officials and employees, to secure and submit the lacking documentary requirements related to the expenses incurred in the subject training/workshop, to facilitate verification of the validity and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Subject the progress payments to retention money equivalent to at least 10 percent of the 50 percent of the value of the completed work for projects that are satisfactorily undertaken and on schedule; otherwise, impose additional retention money on the remaining progress payments equivalent to 10 percent;
Require the contractors to submit a valid financial instrument equivalent to the retention money in case they request its substitution which will only be accepted if the project is on schedule and satisfactorily undertaken;
Ensure proper monitoring and enforcement of contract clauses, particularly to withhold retention money for each progress payment and retain such until the project is finished and the contractor has addressed any defects or issues identified.
Direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Require the contractor to post warranty security that will address the structural defects that may be discovered from the final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed; and
Furnish the Audit Team copy of Certificate of Final Acceptance for every completed contract for monitoring of the said warranty security by the contractors.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the BPLO, to:
Cause the collection of business taxes from the identified contractors who had entered into contracts for infrastructure projects but not registered within the municipality applying the situs of taxation amounting to ₱124,668.64. Likewise, initiate the collection of the deficient business tax amounting to ₱4,648.03 from the contractor that under declared its gross receipts; and
Strictly observe the Municipal Ordinance enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan by thoroughly examining the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of all taxpayers by considering all available evidence and records to ensure that tax assessment is accurate and to avoid non-collection of business taxes that should have been earned by the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to observe the release of barangay shares from RPT collections, including interest thereon to the barangays within five days after the end each quarter;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure timely submission of collection reports to the Accounting Office to facilitate the processing of the RPT shares of the barangays and ensure timely remittance/release thereof; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly schedule showing the computation and the amount of RPT payable to each barangay and record the same in the individual subsidiary ledgers to facilitate the release of RPT shares of the barangays.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to require all suppliers, contractors, and service providers to issue only the prescribed Invoice as the principal evidence of payment, in strict compliance with Revenue Regulation No. 7-2024, dated March 22, 2024. This will ensure that all disbursements are properly supported by valid documentation, consistent with the current BIR regulations.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements are supported by complete, valid, and compliant documentation in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure proper withholding of applicable creditable income tax from the honorarium granted to the abovementioned individuals and remit the same to the BIR; and
Require the designated resource speakers to submit the necessary documents before the payment of honoraria.

We recommended that Management direct the MDRRMO and other concerned officials and personnel to:
Stop charging expenses not directly related to DRRM activities;
Include, under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF, subject to the approval of the LDRRMC, LDC, and Sangguniang Bayan through a formal resolution to ensure additional funding for essential and necessary DRRM PPAs; and
Submit the properly endorsed monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day following the end of each month to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to provide monthly report to the Municipal Accountant showing the list of delinquent stallholders and their corresponding unpaid balances; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Record the unrecognized unpaid rentals in the books of the LGU as of year-end as a debit to Operating Lease Receivable and credit to Rent Income in the amount of ₱25,968.00; and
Strictly adhere to the accrual method of accounting by properly recognizing the unpaid balances of delinquent stallholders including penalties and interest thereon in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
List of delinquent stallholders along with their corresponding unpaid balances for the month was provided by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant.

The Municipal Accountant recorded the unrecognized unpaid rentals in the books of the accounts.

The Municipal Accountant committed to strictly adhere to the accrual method of accounting relative to the recognition of unpaid balances of delinquent stallholders including penalties and interest thereon in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Accrual of unrecognized balances of delinquent stallholders as of December 31, 2023 and September 2024 was taken up in the books as shown in JEV No.100-06-30-001 and 100-09-30-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert back to the GF the amount of ₱43,383.00 representing the unexpended balance of CO which shall be available for use in the risk reduction and management activities;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the reported balance in the SCBAA under Actual Amounts column for LDRRMF – MOOE to present fairly the balances of the appropriations as of December 31, 2023; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to make a follow up of the approval of SB Resolution to facilitate the opening of a separate bank account designated for the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF under the STF.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant reverted back to the General Fund the amount representing the unexpended balance of CO.

Review of the submitted SCBAA for CY 2024 revealed substantial compliance to the audit recommendation.

Management submitted copy of the approved SB Resolution authorizing Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to open Special Trust Fund account for the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Validation revealed compliance to the audit recommendation.

Direct the HRMO to:

Separately present in the payrolls the payment for subsistence and laundry allowances to MHWs; and
Ensure that subsistence and laundry allowances of MHWs are computed based on the prescribed guidelines.

Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

Submit the lacking DTRs to support the payment of subsistence and laundry allowances; and

b.	Submit the lacking documents and/or copies of official receipts in case of refunds to fully liquidate those expenses with insufficient liquidations;
c.	Stop the practice of granting donations and/or payment of advertisements to other LGUs relative to celebration of fiestas and to various religious groups; and
d.	Stop the practice of paying membership and similar fees paid for personal or individual membership in a private organization.

Instruct the MDRRMO to stop charging expenses to the LDRRMF which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;

Require the MDRRMO to refrain from requiring the MDRRMO Personnel to render overtime service except when extremely necessary and they should not be allowed to render overnight service for more than two consecutive nights pursuant to Section 10.3 of Joint Circular CSC-DBM No.1 S.2015; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify correct the Monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in accordance with Section 5.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
Overtime services were no longer charged in the LDRRMF.




MDRRMO Personnel were only allowed to render overtime service when extremely necessary.




Monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM certified correct by the Municipal Accountant were submitted to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory and
render report thereon as required under Sections 119 and 124, Volume
I of Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs,
and to regularly monitor the progress of the inventory taking;
b. Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the
Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports with
the accounting records; and
c. Require the General Services Officer - Designate and Municipal
Accountant to maintain property cards and property, plant and
equipment ledger cards, respectively, and update them periodically to
aid in the conduct of physical count and facilitate the reconciliation of
inventory reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
public infrastructures specially the local road networks;
b. Instruct the General Services Officer - Designate to periodically
render Report on Local Road Networks, and furnish copies to the
concerned offices. Also, the Municipal Engineer should provide the
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer – Designate
with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary
ledgers for each individual road networks; and
d. Ensure coordination among LGU Offices to properly implement the
accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset
Management System.

We reiterated the previous years’ recommendation that Management
should:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to enforce liquidation of cash
advances of the Municipal Officials and Employees concerned on the
prescribed frequency and impose sanctions by withholding their
salaries pursuant to Sections 4.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No.
97-002; and
b. Exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of the accountable officers,
send demand letters and cause the liquidation of the long outstanding
cash advances in compliance with the existing rules and regulations.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to enforce the liquidation of cash
advances of the municipal employees concerned within the prescribed
frequency and impose sanctions by withholding their salaries pursuant
to Sections 4.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of the accountable officers
who were already separated from service and cause the liquidation of
the long outstanding cash advances in compliance with the existing
rules and regulations; and
c. Request for write-off for the outstanding cash advance of the
accountable officer who was already deceased and whose cash advance
remained dormant for more than ten (10) years pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of using the 20%
Development Fund for programs, projects and activities not directly related to
developmental projects and do not constitute the nature of investment or capital
expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents
to support the distribution of the donated goods/materials and to
provide additional documents to determine the purpose/objective of
36
the donations to the recipients; and
b. Advise the SB Members to stop from granting/ giving donations in the
form of cash and in kind pursuant to the provision of Section 447(a) of
RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer to stop the practice
of using the LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities not directly related to
pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations and to
ensure the maximum utilization of the said funds which shall align with the
LDDRMFIP and in accordance with the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that Management include in the funding sources of its
LDRRMFIP the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of prior years in order for the
LGU to maximize the funding and utilization of the calamity fund in compliance
with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002 and to prepare revised Annual
Investment Plan (AIP) in the utilization of the unexpended balances of the
LDRRMF as required under Section 6.5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.
2013-1.

We recommended that Management immediately transfer all the
unexpended/unobligated balances of 2019 QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE to
the Trust Fund books for easy monitoring of the utilization of the LDRRMF and
to ensure availability of the fund to support disaster risk and reduction and
management activities of LDRRMC.

We recommended that Management allocate the required 1% of IRA for
the LCPC as provided under Section l5 of RA 9344, and to direct the designated
members of the LCPC to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and
Local Investment Plan for Children, and to maximize utilization of the allocated
LCPC Fund to the identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No. 2012-120 to
ensure full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to submit all the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers together with
the supporting documents to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the
books and to submit the same to the office of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to submit immediately the collection accounts and disbursement
vouchers including its supporting documents, and to strictly observe the deadline
set for the submission of trial balances and financial reports as required under
Sections 122 and 127 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-
006. We further recommended that Management cause the suspension of
payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the delayed submission
or non-submission of the required reports until the same shall have been
complied in pursuance with Section 127 of the same Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate
retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered lots owned
by the Municipality to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with
the Land Registration Authority in accordance with Section 39 (2) of PD 1445
and Section 449, Volume I of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual to
establish absolute ownership and to protect the interest of the Municipality.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate to exert effort in locating the
documents pertaining to the unidentified value of land recorded in the Financial
Statements and to maintain and update Real Property Ledger Cards for all real
properties owned by the LGU to comply with the pertinent guidelines prescribed
in NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC
Chairman to furnish copies of perfected contract and each of all the documents
forming part thereof to the Audit Team within five working days upon perfection
of the contract for review and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal
Accountant certified list of taxpayer and the amount collectible which
shall serve as basis in establishing Real Property Tax and Special
Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year and to
recognize in the books the amount of collectibles from real property
taxes both for General Fund and Special Education Fund in
accordance with Section 20 of MNGAS to reflect the actual
collectibles from real property taxes in the financial statements of the
Municipality; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to evaluate
the current system and contemplate on acquiring a new software for a
more efficient and effective administration of the real property tax to
come up with the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the
amount due and collectible to provide accurate basis in the recording
of the RPT/SET Receivables; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and officials concerned to maximize
their effort in collecting the amount due in the real property tax and
special education tax receivables to generate additional income for
economic development of the LGU.

Except for petty cash fund, require the Accountable Officer to refund any unexpended balance to the cashier/ collecting officer, who will issue the necessary official receipt.

As to the cash advances of former officials and employees of the municipality who resigned, retired, and transferred, we  have recommend further, that Management should be persistent in sending demand letters and file appropriate legal action, if warranted to those who have filed to liquidate the overdue cash advances including those who are no longer in the government service.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously calls the attention of employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Most of officers and employees who has unliquidated cash advances already lost the supporting document to liquidate their expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management adopt a strict policy to regulate the utilization of Telephone-Communication Allowance, to limit the charging of mobile telephone expenses to government funds in order to prevent incurrence of excessive mobile phone expenses. Contracts for mobile plan should be entered into the name of the Municipality and not to the name of end-users. Finally, the Municipal Accountant should verify that all basic documentary requirements were attached before processing the payment.
observations.managementAction: Budget officer has been more strict in releasing funds for excessive telephone bills.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and proper planning on its utilization should be made so that all programmed development projects during the year could be fully implemented for the essential benefits to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Funds have been utilized for projects beneficial to the people of Basista.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of procuring of materials, supplies and equipment through reimbursement basis. In all instances, all procurement of goods and services shall be course through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), which in turn consolidate the requisitions of all Departments for a quarter and shall be done through public bidding.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the employees of this ruling and implemented it accordingly.
observations.reason: Emergency purchases and procurement to suppliers who do not accept check payments are unavoidable in important and for some practical reasons.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management thru the initiative of the Honorable Municipal Mayor close its bank account with the PPSB and to transfer all funds and cash balances to an authorized government depository bank or GFIs which are classified as those prescribed under law and which has a Camels rating of at least “3”. Within one (1) year from the effectivity of the Circular to ensure that taxpayer’s money are safeguarded to the fullest extent and its optimal use.
observations.managementAction: PPSB account has been closed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC ensure to prepare individual BAC Resolution for purchases under alternative mode of procurement as required under the above-mentioned COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: BAC prepares individual BAC Resolution for purchase under alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-002, and instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF for the submission to the Office of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) and to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and to create the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) and to designate a Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) to administer the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Reports are prepared and submitted
